zh.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield是Elias de Giffard和Ala d'Evreaux的兒子，於1100年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德。他於大約1127年與Berthe fitzPons在英國格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1159年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德享年59歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield是Elias de Giffard和Ala d'Evreaux的儿子，于1100年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德。他于大约1127年与Berthe fitzPons在英国格盧斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1159年在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德享年59岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Giffard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Continental Germanic personal name Gifard. (ii) nickname, perhaps sometimes from Old French giffard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Guiver
meanings: English: (i) nickname, apparently from Middle English giver ‘giver, donor, bestower’. (ii) variant of Giffard with voicing of /f/ to /v/ and loss of final -d.

variants.name: Gafford
meanings: English (southeastern): assimilated form of Gatford, a name of various origins. In Sussex and Surrey it is perhaps a variant of the habitational name Catford from a place so called in Somerset. Gatford is also from the habitational names Gateford (in Worksop, Nottinghamshire) or Gateforth (near Selby, Yorkshire), both formed with Old English gāt ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’, influenced by Old Norse geit ‘goat’.

Probably also an altered form of German Gaffert: habitational name from Gaffert near Köslin, Brandenburg, or from a personal name formed with Middle High German gate ‘fellow, companion’. The surname Gaffert is very rare in Germany.

English: perhaps an altered form of Gatewood , an occupational name for a ‘goatherd’ (Middle English gate-werde, Old English gātweard) or for a ‘gate keeper’ (Middle English gateward, Old English ʒeatweard, dialect gātward).

variants.name: Gifford
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

nameMeanings.name: II,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Elias
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from the Greek form (used in the New Testament) of the name of the prophet   Elijah .  It is one of a number of Old Testament names which have come back into favour since the latter part of the 20th century.  See also  Ellis .

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield was born in 1100, in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England as the son of Elias de Giffard and Ala d'Evreaux. He married Berthe fitzPons about 1127, in Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1159, in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59, and was buried in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield est né en 1100, à Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, fils d’Elias de Giffard et Ala d'Evreaux. Il a épousé Berthe fitzPons environ 1127, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1159, à Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield wurde 1100 in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England, als Sohn von Elias de Giffard und Ala d'Evreaux geboren. Er hat um 1127 in Gloucestershire, England Berthe fitzPons geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1159 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield è nato il nel 1100 nel Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England come figlio di Elias de Giffard e Ala d'Evreaux. Lui sposato Berthe fitzPons circa 1127, nel Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1159, nel Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfieldは1100年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリンプスフィールドでElias de GiffardとAla d'Evreauxの息子として生まれた。約1127年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでBerthe fitzPonsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1159年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリンプスフィールドで59歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリンプスフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield(은)는 1100년 영국 그루스스터실르 브림프스필드에서 Elias de Giffard(와)과 Ala d'Evreaux의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1127년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 Berthe fitzPons(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1159년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 브림프스필드에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 브림프스필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield nasceu em 1100, em Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra filho de Elias de Giffard e Ala d'Evreaux. Ele casou-se com Berthe fitzPons aproximadamente 1127, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1159, em Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield родился в 1100 г. в местности бримпсфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия. Его родителей звали Elias de Giffard и Ala d'Evreaux. Он женился на женщине по имени Berthe fitzPons около 1127 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1159 г. в местности бримпсфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности бримпсфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Elias Giffard  II, Lord of Brimpsfield nació en 1100, en Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra como hijo de Elias de Giffard y Ala d'Evreaux. Se casó con Berthe fitzPons aproximadamente 1127, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1159, en Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
